methodology: Every entry verified against live source code and, where marked liveVerified:true, against a running local server (JWT_SECRET set locally, PORT 5092-5099, never a shared/production instance). Categories are feature-level, not raw file counts. A feature can only be REAL if it performs genuine external I/O or genuine internal product work that survives restart; DUPLICATE is reserved for near-identical re-implementations of the same capability; SIMULATED means the code runs for real but only mutates self-generated, self-contained JSON state with no connection to external reality.

features.evidence: backend/middleware/authMiddleware.js:11-40 — HS256, crypto.timingSafeEqual constant-time compare, expiry enforced, fail-closed dev bypass (requires NODE_ENV!==production AND ALLOW_DEV_AUTH_BYPASS=1, else 503).
features.notes: Re-verified live this session: no-cookie->401, bad-token->401, valid-token->200.

features.evidence: backend/middleware/firebaseAuth.js — fully real requireAuth/optionalAuth using real firebase-admin SDK and Firebase ID-token verification. grep -rln 'firebaseAuth' backend/ (excluding the file itself) returns zero results — never imported by any route.
features.notes: Not a stub — genuinely working code, but unreachable. Duplicate auth mechanism relative to authMiddleware.js's cookie-JWT scheme, never wired.

features.evidence: backend/services/organizationService.cjs:138 _assertPermission() enforced inside every mutation (updateOrg, deleteOrg, addMember, etc — lines 227,241,277,295,309,328,353,369,397,424,442,471,487,508). Route-layer orgMiddleware.cjs (attachOrg/requireOrgPermission) now also mounted in backend/routes/organizations.js as of this session's fix.
features.notes: FIXED THIS SESSION (2 bugs): (1) prior session's attachOrg id-forwarding used req.query.orgId, which Express 5 exposes as a getter that does not persist mutations across middleware — silently never worked, fixed to use req.body.orgId. (2) Full 5-role (owner/admin/dept_lead/member/viewer) matrix re-verified live end-to-end after the fix: update_org/delete_org/manage_members/create_mission/cross-org-isolation all correct.

features.evidence: backend/middleware/workspaceMiddleware.cjs — attachWorkspace/requireWorkspaceMember/requireRole real. backend/services/workspaceService.cjs:132,166 enforce role checks inside updateWorkspace/createInvitation. GET /workspace/:id/members (backend/routes/workspace.js) previously had ZERO membership check.
features.notes: FIXED THIS SESSION: GET /workspace/:id/members returned any workspace's member list (names+emails, real PII) to any authenticated user regardless of membership. Wired requireWorkspaceMember via the same req.body-forwarding adapter pattern. Live-verified: owner->200, non-member outsider->403.

features.evidence: backend/routes/index.js — requireAuth now mounted per-prefix immediately before each router.use(require(...)) line.
features.notes: Fixed in prior session (security/hardening-p0, commit a262db7), re-verified live this session still holding after further changes: all 33 target endpoints return 401 without a session.

features.evidence: backend/services/featureGate.cjs:115 (pre-fix) read only req.user?.accountId || req.user?.id; the actual JWT claim used everywhere else in this codebase (authMiddleware.js signJWT/verifyJWT, billingService.js:284) is req.user.sub. Same bug independently present in backend/routes/{aiEcosystem,growthOS,creativeStudio,launchPlatform,browserPlatform,commercial,contentSEO}.js.
features.notes: FIXED THIS SESSION across 8 files (featureGate.cjs + 7 route files) — every real logged-in user was being treated as unauthenticated by every requireFeature()-gated route (hard 401 on e.g. /marketplace/catalog) or silently bucketed into a shared 'unknown'/'global' account. Live-verified: /marketplace/catalog now correctly returns 402 feature_gated/requires_starter_or_higher for a real trial user instead of a false 401.

features.evidence: backend/services/aiService.js:58-95 — 12 real provider endpoints (Groq/OpenAI/OpenRouter/Anthropic/Gemini/DeepSeek/Together/Fireworks/Cohere/NVIDIA/Ollama/LM Studio), real axios HTTP, TCP local-probe fail-fast, documented failover order.
features.notes: Live-verified this session: /ai/status 200 with real provider list; raw GROQ reachability confirmed ~350-450ms round trip with a real key. App-level /ai/chat is rate-limit-fragile under burst (full-provider-loop + retry amplifies request count against GROQ's per-minute limit) — real but fragile, not fake.

features.evidence: backend/services/secretVault.cjs — real AES-256-GCM, key=SHA-256(JWT_SECRET). Wired to real callers (integrationConnectors.cjs:78, founderIdentityOS.cjs). Live /vault/dashboard and /vault/health return real per-connector rotation/health data (3 real secrets, score 100).
backend/services/oauthIntegrationLayer.cjs:264 (exchange), :325 (refresh), :342 (revoke) — real authorization-code flow over raw https for google/github/slack/notion/microsoft/linkedin.
features.notes: Confirmed by Phase 3 sub-audit. Caveat: Google/Microsoft/LinkedIn 'CONNECTED' health probes hit a PUBLIC discovery endpoint (integrationConnectors.cjs:709) that returns 200 regardless of whether the app's own client_id/secret are valid — proves the vendor is reachable, not that this app's OAuth credentials work.

features.feature: Connectors: GitHub, Telegram, WhatsApp, Razorpay, OpenAI, email/Resend-SendGrid-Postmark-Mailgun, S3/R2 storage, Sentry
features.evidence: See docs/current/phase3-connector-verification.md. Telegram + OpenAI live-CONNECTED with real keys in .env (bot @Alwaliy_Technologies_Jarvis_Bot verified, OpenAI /v1/models succeeded). GitHub/WhatsApp/Razorpay have real code paths (api.github.com, graph.facebook.com, api.razorpay.com) that returned real non-2xx statuses live because no/incomplete credentials are configured in this environment — code is real, not stubbed.

features.feature: Connectors: Slack, Discord, Stripe, Firebase, Supabase, Cloudflare, AWS, Anthropic, Gemini
features.evidence: See docs/current/phase3-connector-verification.md. All have real vendor-host HTTP probes and/or real SDK usage but no credentials present in .env; Firebase's probe never makes an outbound call at all (JSON-parses its own service-account env var).
features.notes: Anthropic and Gemini keys are fully real end-to-end in code but ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/GEMINI_API_KEY were absent from .env.example — FIXED THIS SESSION (added to .env.example so a by-the-book deploy is told to set them).

features.feature: Connectors: Gmail, Google Calendar, Google Drive
features.evidence: Ride Google OAuth scopes only (oauthIntegrationLayer.cjs:110). Zero outbound calls anywhere in source to gmail.googleapis.com, googleapis.com/calendar, or googleapis.com/drive. Status reported is scope-declared + 'pending user auth', never a real API round trip.
features.notes: Reports PARTIAL/READY in the connector dashboard despite no actual Gmail/Calendar/Drive API call existing in the codebase.

features.evidence: Zero code anywhere in backend/services or backend/routes. Not present in the 57-connector registry, no env vars, no .env.example entries.
features.notes: Named in this mission's Phase 3 scope but never built at all — not partial, not broken, simply absent.

features.evidence: backend/routes/mission.js, backend/services/missionOrchestrator.cjs, missionMemory.cjs — real fs persistence to data/*.json, survives restart. Live /mission/runtime/status returned 228 real missions with status/priority breakdown.

backend/routes/pipeline.js live-tested: /pipeline/active returns 200 with real (empty at test time) pipeline list — real code path, not fake, but only MANUAL-ONLY (fires when POST /pipeline/run is called, not on its own timer).

backend/services/engineeringMemoryEngine.cjs:124-157 (remember, real branches for type=lesson and type=mission_learning, real persistence via continuousLearningEngine/missionMemory) and :163+ (recall, real TF-IDF-style cosine similarity search across 5 real memory sources).
features.notes: Live-verified: POST /memory/remember with type:lesson returned stored:true with a real generated lessonId; type:decision correctly and intentionally returns stored:false (documented — decisions derive from a separate pipeline, this is not a bug).

features.evidence: See docs/current/phase6-autonomous-runtime.md. Live 70s idle-window observation with zero API calls showed: real execSync(git status)/exec(pm2 jlist) firing autonomously (continuousRuntimeObserver.cjs), 80 autonomous dispatch batches making 1585 real outbound HTTP calls to AI providers (agents/autonomousLoop.cjs), and a self-heal probe that detected 5 real failed cycles and re-executed them live.
features.notes: Genuinely autonomous — fires with zero human trigger and produces real external side effects (network calls), not just JSON mutation.

features.evidence: continuousLearningEngine.cjs:345-351 (real 30-min timer, real fs-backed clustering) and agentRuntimeSupervisor.cjs:948 (real 60s planner tick) both fire autonomously with zero human action, confirmed live.
features.notes: Triggers and code are real, but the data consumed is largely the system's own historical output (data/agent-runs.json — its own prior failures), not fresh external-world signal. Self-monitoring, not learning from the outside world. At boot, Learning produced 0 new lessons (already exhausted its static input).

features.feature: 14 duplicate *Org engines (businessOrg, autonomousOrg, civilizationOrg, ecosystemOrg, enterpriseOrg, executiveOrg, autonomousEvolutionOrg, autonomousKnowledgeOrg, platformOrg + *State/*Workflow siblings)
features.evidence: docs/current/service-classification-report.json (34 files flagged SIMULATION, largely this family). organizationRegistryEngine.cjs explicitly documents itself as a federation layer over these same engines. Each follows an identical registry+tick pattern.

docs/current/phase6-autonomous-runtime.md Caveat 2 — confirmed 0 setInterval/cron/node-cron calls in any of the 9 core *Org engine files; they only run when an API route calls them (MANUAL-ONLY) or an AgentSupervisor persona tick invokes them. Where a persona tick does fire them, 100% of resulting actions are self-contained data/*.json mutations — zero calls to a real connector or real repo/infra modification found.
features.notes: Live-confirmed this run, not just by static grep — the 70s idle-window trace showed *Org persona ticks firing and only emitting internal events + JSON writes.

features.feature: Frontend: DeveloperOS, PersonalOS, EnterpriseOS, MemoryOSV2 tabs
features.evidence: docs/current/phase4-frontend-audit.md — developerApi.js -> /dev/*, personalApi.js -> /personal/*, enterpriseApi.js -> /enterprise/*: no backend route file exists for any of these three prefixes anywhere in backend/routes/. Live probes of /dev/repos, /personal/dashboard, /enterprise/orgs all return the HTML SPA shell, not JSON.
features.notes: Not fixed — building 3 entire new backend namespaces is new architecture/new features, explicitly out of scope for this mission. These 4 top-level tabs render as permanently empty shells with no visible error to the user.

features.feature: Frontend: EnterpriseCRM, KnowledgeCenter, DataOwnershipCenter, DisasterRecoveryCenter, MobilePlatformCenter, CommunityCenter, AutonomousCompanyCenter, AutonomousRevenueCenter, AutonomousMarketingCenter, AutonomousSupportCenter
features.evidence: docs/current/phase4-frontend-audit.md rows — each renders hardcoded const arrays (e.g. DATA_INVENTORY with literal record counts, BACKUPS/RECOVERY_PLANS, named fake leads 'Arjun Mehta, Priya Sharma') as if they were live operational data, with zero backend API call.
features.notes: The 4 Autonomous*Center pages are also a DUPLICATE cluster — ~240 lines each, ~90% identical structure, mirroring the backend *Org duplication pattern.

features.feature: Frontend pages with real backend wiring (~50 of ~69 audited tabs)
features.evidence: docs/current/phase4-frontend-audit.md full table — Dashboard, CRM/Payments, Chat, Mission Control, Billing, Workspace Settings, Team, Marketplace, Engineering/Runtime centers, AI cost center, etc. all confirmed calling real, existing backend routes with JSON responses.

backend/routes/tasks.js:28-33 requires agents/metrics/metricsCollector.cjs, which does not exist in the live agents/ tree — only a copy survives under _archive/20260520_010917/agents/metrics/metricsCollector.cjs. The route degrades gracefully (503 'Metrics collector unavailable') rather than crashing, but the feature has never worked in the current tree.
features.notes: Not fixed — resurrecting archived code without knowing why it was archived risks reintroducing a deliberately-removed component; reported precisely instead. Narrow blast radius: /scheduler/status uses a different, working code path (taskQueueMod) and is unaffected.

features.evidence: grep -rln 'db/sqlite' backend/ (excluding the file itself) returns zero results — required by nothing in the live server path. better-sqlite3 is a real dependency with correct, parameterized (non-SQLi) query code, but it is never invoked in production, and data/jarvis.db does not exist in this environment.
features.notes: The native module ABI mismatch (compiled for NODE_MODULE_VERSION 145, this Node needs 137) was FIXED THIS SESSION via `npm rebuild better-sqlite3`, resolving repeated non-fatal '[SQLite Shadow]' warnings — but the module remains unwired/dead regardless.

features.evidence: Unconditionally assumed a snapshot always contains jarvis.db and crashed with ENOENT when it didn't — because safe-backup.cjs only writes jarvis.db when a live SQLite DB exists to back up (it doesn't, in this or presumably most environments — see sqlite dead-code entry above), and falls back to jarvis.db.raw on VACUUM-INTO failure.
features.notes: FIXED THIS SESSION: script now checks for either jarvis.db or jarvis.db.raw in the snapshot, and skips gracefully (with a clear log line) if neither is present rather than assuming one always exists. Live-verified: `node scripts/test-restore.cjs` now exits 0 / 'PASSED' (was previously a hard crash).

features.evidence: docs/current/phase7-9-performance-devops.md — safe-backup.cjs live-run created a real tar.gz snapshot and pruned old ones (exit 0). rollback.sh --list live-run listed 8 real data backups and 10 recent git commits.
features.notes: Destructive restore paths (data/ wipe+restore, --code git-checkout) were not exercised live in the DevOps sub-audit beyond what test-restore.cjs already covers, since they require a disposable target.

features.evidence: docs/current/phase7-9-performance-devops.md — pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs live-verified both apps reach 'online' with no crash-loop in the observed window; single-entry config confirmed (no EADDRINUSE risk).
features.notes: jarvis-os shows 590 cumulative historical restarts (did not increment during this test run) — flagged by the app's own pm2Observer as HIGH; root cause of the historical accumulation not investigated in this pass.

features.evidence: docs/current/phase7-9-performance-devops.md — every command CI actually runs was executed locally and passed: test:runtime 144/144, build:frontend (7.2M output), deploy-script bash -n syntax checks on all 8 scripts.
features.notes: Real GitHub Actions execution not directly triggerable from this environment; local command-for-command proxy is the strongest available substitute and all of it passed.

features.evidence: docs/current/phase7-9-performance-devops.md — Docker daemon unavailable in this environment (90s wait, did not come up), so the image was not actually built this run. Static multi-stage review found no defects: cross-stage COPY paths match, non-root user, healthcheck present.
features.notes: Statically correct by inspection but not proven by an actual build in this pass.

features.evidence: scripts/electron-smoke-test.cjs live-run: 22/22 checks passed against the actually-loaded electron/main.cjs — contextIsolation:true, nodeIntegration:false, webSecurity not overridden to false, CSP header injection, will-navigate guard, setPermissionRequestHandler all confirmed present in source.
features.notes: Corrects a prior-session error that had cited these properties from the wrong file (electron/main.js, not the actually-loaded main.cjs).

features.feature: Electron full GUI launch / workspace load timing
features.evidence: docs/current/phase7-9-performance-devops.md — a real, bounded launch attempt of electron/main.cjs exited in ~483ms with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (isPackaged)' because the Electron app global isn't initialized in a non-interactive/no-display invocation.
features.notes: Marked UNUSED here in the sense of 'not exercisable in this audit environment', not a code defect — the smoke test (static) is the only available signal; this is an environment limitation of the audit, not a proven app bug.

features.evidence: backend/middleware/rateLimiter.js is real (token-bucket style) and applied on 7 of 127 route files: accounts.js, auth.js, browser.js, jarvis.js, odi.js, runtime.js, whatsapp.js.
features.notes: The two highest-risk endpoints for brute-force/spam (login, registration) are covered. Narrow overall coverage (7/127 files) is a real, reportable gap, not a fabricated one.

features.evidence: No dedicated CSRF-token middleware exists anywhere in backend/. Auth cookie is sameSite:'strict' (authMiddleware.js:93), which is a real, meaningful (if partial) mitigation against cross-site state-changing requests using the auth cookie.
features.notes: grep hits for 'csrf' in backend/services/{engineeringReasoningEngine,pcsCredentials}.cjs are the security-linter's own detection-rule definitions and OAuth-layer self-audit checks, not application CSRF protection — verified as false leads, not counted as coverage.

features.evidence: grep -rc dangerouslySetInnerHTML frontend/src --include=*.jsx returns zero non-zero hits — no raw HTML injection sink found in the audited component set.

features.feature: selfHealingFrontend.cjs applyFix() — path handling + new Function()
features.evidence: backend/services/selfHealingFrontend.cjs:156-179 — path.join(process.cwd(), record.targetFile) with no traversal sanitization, and a new Function(patched) syntax-guard. grep -rn 'applyFix\(' backend/ (excluding the definition) returns zero call sites anywhere in the backend.
features.notes: Real path-traversal-shaped and eval-adjacent code pattern, but currently unreachable from any route — reported as dead code with a latent risk noted, not treated as an active production vulnerability. Not fixed: modifying unreferenced code was judged out of scope (no live exposure to hardening).

features.evidence: backend/db/sqlite.cjs uses only parameterized/static .prepare() calls (e.g. SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM tasks) — no string-concatenated queries found. (Also DEAD_CODE per above — unreachable regardless.)
features.notes: Listed separately from the dead-code entry because 'is the query-construction pattern itself safe' is a distinct question from 'is this code reachable' — answer to both happens to be favorable here.
